body:
### Describe the bug

I am trying to enable Cython cache (https://github.com/cython/cython/pull/6091) at PyArrow and have found the following error when re-running compilation a second time hence when hitting the cache:
```
  FAILED: [code=1] CMakeFiles/arrow_python.dir/pyarrow/src/arrow/python/pyarrow.cc.o
  /usr/bin/x86_64-linux-gnu-g++ -DARROW_HAVE_RUNTIME_AVX2 -DARROW_HAVE_RUNTIME_AVX512 -DARROW_HAVE_RUNTIME_BMI2 -DARROW_HAVE_RUNTIME_SSE4_2 -DARROW_HAVE_SSE4_2 -DARROW_PYTHON_EXPORTING -Darrow_python_EXPORTS -I/home/raulcd/code/arrow/python/pyarrow/src -I/tmp/tmp7015iv4v/build/pyarrow/src -I/usr/include/python3.13 -I/home/raulcd/code/dist/include -I/home/raulcd/code/pyarrow-dev/lib/python3.13/site-packages/numpy/_core/include -Wno-noexcept-type -Wno-self-move  -Wall -fno-semantic-interposition -msse4.2  -fdiagnostics-color=always  -fno-omit-frame-pointer -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-maybe-uninitialized -g -O0  -std=c++20 -fPIC -MD -MT CMakeFiles/arrow_python.dir/pyarrow/src/arrow/python/pyarrow.cc.o -MF CMakeFiles/arrow_python.dir/pyarrow/src/arrow/python/pyarrow.cc.o.d -o CMakeFiles/arrow_python.dir/pyarrow/src/arrow/python/pyarrow.cc.o -c /home/raulcd/code/arrow/python/pyarrow/src/arrow/python/pyarrow.cc
  In file included from /home/raulcd/code/arrow/python/pyarrow/src/arrow/python/pyarrow.cc:32:
  /home/raulcd/code/arrow/python/pyarrow/src/arrow/python/pyarrow_api.h:19:10: fatal error: arrow/python/lib_api.h: No such file or directory
     19 | #include "arrow/python/lib_api.h"
        |          ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
  compilation terminated.

```
After some investigation I think this has to do with:
https://github.com/cython/cython/blob/eee7a607684485e2feb0177da4ea621daf9f1d5c/Cython/Build/Cache.py#L154-L155

I've been doing some debugging and this is what my temporary location looks like on the extract:
```
├── lib_api.h
│   └── lib_api.h
├── lib.cpp
│   └── lib.cpp
├── lib.h
│   └── lib.h
```
As you can see `z.extract(artifact, join_path(dirname, artifact))` is storing my artifact into a folder with the artifact name `/tmp/tmp7015iv4v/build/lib_api.h/lib_api.h` and fails locating my artifacts.
I've tested locally modifying the above line to `z.extract(artifact, dirname)` and compilation when hitting the cache works as expected.

Is there any reason for that path that I am missing?

### Code to reproduce the behaviour:

Just enabling cache on PyArrow builds, I am unsure on how to build a minimal reproducer for this but adding a line to our build system.
```
list(APPEND CYTHON_FLAGS "--cache")
```


### Expected behaviour

The compilation works when hitting the cache and it does not fail.

### OS

Tested on Linux

### Python version

3.13.2

### Cython version

3.2.4

### Additional context

I've validated the code is still the same on main.

comment_data.body: I am happy to submit a PR if the issue seems valid to the maintainers unless is just that I am missing something on our side.
The proposed fix looks right to me so a PR would be welcome. Superficially it looks like we only current test it using gzip files (but I haven't searched very thoroughly)

title: [BUG] Cython cache seems to extracts files to wrong folder when finding zip cache files
